product_title: Echo Show 8 (2nd Gen, 2021 release) - Glacier White - with 30-day FREE trial of Alexa Together (monthly auto-renewal)
combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: Big Disappointment- Gen 2 Echo8 is a Nightmare! Text: This was purchased to replace an Echo 5 that I loved, but had been bricked after the latest over -the- air update from Amazon. It was ruined by the update and never recovered. This one is not anything like my original. There is a giant digital clock that I cannot change… the old echo five had a setting for this- the background adjustment control has also disappeared! Now it is called clock and photo display- and there is NO setting for background! I already had a photo for my background in my Alexa app and added it to the new Echo 8- but it does not show as a background choice now, just part of a rotating photo display, which in my case I do not want. I am unable to get the background to stay on instead it disappears and the only thing showing is the giant clock numbers which I do NOT want to see! I tried every setting but as I said the settings in new version removed any background and clock setting as was available in the old Alexa app and on screen settings. There are constant rotations of things even though I have turned off everything or told this new Echo to hide them- I just want the screen to show my background photo and the little clock in the top left corner like my old echo5 did. The new one insists on showing me recipes ( I Don’t cook) or a song that I played weeks and weeks ago and don’t want to see or hear about UNTIL I ask the Echo for it. Finally, I had to unplug this thing last night as the screen would not and I repeat, would not ever remove that darn clock! It is pretty big and there is no setting to make it smaller or dimmer- the new low power mode ( which I also do not want) dims the screen, removes my backgound picture ( which I want left up) and lo and behold, what is left glaringly bright on the screen - that digital clock in bright white big numbers! If I wanted a digital clock I would have purchased one. Asking this Echo to do the things I used to ask on my old little show5 is not intuitive at all and instead made slower and more difficult. I was able to find a background setting on an older ipad which had a previous version of the alexa app- Just an FYI, I used that to add my background pix and it still would not stay on the screen of this new model- and as I said, the old settings on both Alexa app and the Echo show itself have been changed as have some other things such as how often the rotation of things you do not want to see appear on the screen. Telling this echo to hide them or removing them in the app had little affect. So I am likely sending it back after I deregister etc.- very, very disappointed… and was quite content before the over the air update broke my original echo show- “sometimes no change is better” is a hint I’ll give to the young developers behind the scenes…
*** In all fairness, Amazon called today after this review went live and did indicate they would pass along my concerns and problems using the new software and settings on this model- appreciated the effort and if I do keep this new device or get another- perhaps an update will come giving users a choice on background and clock as well as the low power mode- choices on settings empower users to control the devices they bought and own… Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Echo Show 5 is better Text: I had an Echo Show 5 (2nd gen) in the kitchen in front of the sink in a bay window so I replaced it with this Echo Show 8.
The first thing I noticed is how heavy it was. It's way too heavy for what it is.
Second dislike was the sound. It's not that much louder than the Echo Show 5 and lacks a lot of highs. Good enough for news but they just have to add some small tweeters to the sides to make the audio a lot clearer. My 3watt $10 computer speakers sound clearer. Voices and bass is good enough but don't expect clear highs from it.
Third dislike is the connection to my firetv recast. The Echo Show 5 I could say "show channel guide" and it would give me a list of shows on now. The Echo Show 8 says I have 2 fire TV recasts and to configure it in the app. The app only shows one fire tv recast. The Echo Show 5 I setup in my office still lets me show channel guide and it will show what's on. I can still watch live tv but I have to know the station name. I can't say "Alexa, tune to CW" or "Alexa, tune to NBC". I have to say "Alexa, tune to the CW" or Alexa, tune to the NBC".
The camera is good when it works. I have to wait 10 seconds before the video shows up when dropping in on it. I think it's doing it's face tracking when the camera starts. The image looks like 64 little scenes at start up and then it eventually goes down to one.

I thought the Echo Show 8 was supposed to function a lot better than a 2nd Gen Echo Show 5 but it doesn't. It's just bigger, heavier, and has a better camera. I didn't expect to have problems working with my Fire TV recast, poor sound, or it being so heavy. Reviews I read before buying didn't mention these issues. Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: This is one of the worst purchases I've ever made Text: Update April 10th: Amazon reached out to me after I made this review and offered to exchange my old Echo Show with a replacement. It worked great for a few days and then the same issues started again (runtime errors). In fact, it's happening more often with the new device they sent me. Basically I have two bricks now and I have to go out of my way to return the first brick while I still have issues with the second brick they sent me. This is beyond ridiculous. Also, Amazon verifies when you are having issues with your device (they can see how many times you receive errors on your devices and how many times you have to do a factory reset) so they are well aware of what is happening to me.

I will try to explain how horrible this item is. I purchased two Echo Shows five months ago and used the monthly payments which was fine (in hindsight if I had to pay for both upfront I never would have purchased them). I have had issues with these devices from day one.

First of all, Echo Show doesn't come with a remote which of course I knew beforehand but I didn't think it was an issue. After all, you just turn on your favorite shows or movies and the autoplay so why would you even need a remote right? Wrong. These devices continously have "runtime" errors, connection errors and playback errors and when this happens your shows and movies stop every time.

So what do you have to do? That's right, scream at Alexa to restart or get up and manually start the program again like it's the 1970s. Where is the convenience in that? The worst part is it used to come with a remote and apparently Amazon thought convenience wasn't important to their customers and discontinued the add-on.

Whenever I contact "support" about these runtime, connection and playback errors everyone chats or speaks to me like I'm speaking an alienic language. The ONLY thing that works is resetting the entire device which means you have to start everything over including your wifi and account login. Who has time for all of that? This has happened to me over 10 times in the last week. Keep in mind this device is only five months old! What I thought was interesting was these problems really ramped up right after I made my final payment and now I'm stuck with this thing.

As far as adding other apps or skills like Hulu, good luck. You can't just pull up a list of skills without scanning the internet for a minimum of 15 minutes trying to figure out how to add it to your device. I think it started working when I enabled the Hulu skill on my Alexa app (don't get me started on that app and the way it lags and just stops working at times) but don't quote me on that because it was almost like a fever dream that I've blocked out; that's how frustrating the process was.

Speaking of Hulu, as I said there are so many errors with Echo Show and Hulu it's ridiculous. Hulu works fine on other devices including Amazon's Fire Tablet but they are at war when the Show is involved. Amazon Prime video works but how many times can you watch Hanna before you're bored out of your mind?

Alexa is pretty decent and understands what you're saying most of the time but you have to be right next to the screen. The sound is also really good and I do appreciate that.

If I could take it back I would. It's a headache and so annoying when it just shuts off sporadically. Next time I'll just stop being cheap and buy a smart TV. At least it comes with a remote. NEUTRAL REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Honestly a bit disappointed. I was expecting more Text: I sort of purchased this on a whim to see if I could fit it into my life somehow. I found the idea of a smart display intriguing as I already have 2 Fire Cubes and absolutely love them. However a Fire cube this is not. It's difficult to express but this thing is cumbersome to use and it's slow. For example. I was eating dinner and watching a video I purchased on Prime video. I had to pause it to get up and do something so I said "echo pause" Wich it did. When I returned the screen had defaulted back to the time home screen. No big deal right? So I said "echo play" and instead of resuming my show, it resumed a song I had paused earlier that day. I asked it to stop and then asked for it to play my show. It began to play a different episode and not where I left off. Getting it back to where I left off required more awkward voice control and touching the screen. Very annoying when I'm trying to eat my meal. This device also has trouble understanding my queries. It's getting my words incorrect. One feature I was pleasently surprised by was the sound quality. It is very good. The low end is impressive. Almost as good as my Nest Audio. Maybe with some software updates this device Will be good. But for now I'm still trying to understand it's intended flow. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Good but can improve a lot Text: I just received this and was really excited for it. I bought this for my room because my roommate often stays up when he gets home to watch his shows, but I refuse to get a TV for my room. Using the other Alexa parts was just a plus for me, honestly.

Positives for me are the sound quality (amazing), voice recognition is better than on my old echo dot, and beautiful display.

Things that I hope Amazon listens to and improves…
- This is sort of difficult to navigate. I’m really good with technology but oh my god I can’t imagine my mother trying to work this thing. I’m okay with it, but it’s irritatingly annoying to use.
- Lots of streaming services are wonky, even the ones that are Amazon based (twitch..????)
- It’s difficult to set certain things up, like connecting my Spotify took a hot minute

Otherwise, I really enjoy it and I hope that the next update Amazon puts out for it helps with some of the navigation and streaming. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: Video quality and sound quality are great. Text: Only thing I don't like is when your watching a movie or something on it and you have to leave it paused for longer than a few minutes, it takes you all the way out of the video and you have to find it all over again. POSITIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: A MUST HAVE! Text: [[VIDEOID:b6537fe65ba9d68258a80970448c9f4a]] Gah! I love how big this screen is!!!! I've been suffering downstairs without an Alexa in my new house and decided decided to Gift myself a housewarming and so glad I did! My son is also loving watching cartoons during breakfast 😏. This speaker is SUPER oud. Definitely all you would need to throw a house party AND have the neighbors knocking 😂. It's chic and pretty! Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: A good system for what it does. Text: Amazon Echo Show 8 review:
Me, it's not what I thought it would be.
Also me, it's not a bad device.
Also, also me, It's not what you'd think.
You can remotely login and watch areas it's in, watch multiple cameras that are connected all over the place, look up content, listen to music (I'm listening to EYE OF THE TIGER) and it pretty much is a KARAOKE system, among other things.
In short, it's a smart speaker (yes, a "SMART SPEAKER"). You can't easily or at all, install third-party applications on it, requires the Alexa App, but if you want to control other Amazon/Alexa devices and such that are compatible, it's not a bad device.
I can control my TV (I will not explain that, too much of a pain in the ass), lights, and other items just from it. It's a great "hub", or a damn good device. For now, it's on sale, and for AP ("Amazon Prime") members, there's a discount.
For recipes, watching videos, among other things, it's a good device. Would I go against buying it? Depends what you're expecting from it, otherwise, it's not a bad investment -- and a good gift, especially for the elderly that want something simple.
I've been using mine for the following:
1. Tracking weather
2. Managing devices (near and far)
3. Monitoring my room when I am not home. (Alexa app required)
4. Listening to music (The sound is great for those that don't mind the best, or care for the best, etc., and better than low-end IMHO).
5. Watching movies (Halloween Ends, GOOD!) But I switched to my T V, because I ended up having to lay down.
6. It DOES NOT COME WITH A BATTERY (extra item sold seprately) and a STAND that is a separate purchase, but does well on it's own otherwise.
If I were to rate, I'd give it a 7 out of 10

I posted this on my personal Facebook, it's a good product. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: From sceptical to IMPRESSED Text: At first I was a bit apprehensive based on feedback from others and watching several YouTube videos about blah blah blah. So i took the plunge and bought one of these just to test it out and see how it performs as I wanted my own experience to be the judge, and boy oh boy, i am officially impressed.

SOUND
The sound is clean and you would not believe the high volume that is coming out of this device. I have mine set to about a little over quarter and it is just good for everything on a daily basis.

SCREEN
I think the 8 inch screen hits the sweet spot, not too big, not too small. Its clear, the touch response is good so far with no complaints.

ALEXA
Damn, that woman can catch her name every time, all the time. She doesn't miss a beat and she probably misunderstood what we asked her to do but after the basics she is quite capable of executing skills.

MY USAGE
I have this device connected to my home security cameras (LOREX) and the integration is very good. I can ask Alexa to view different cameras and I'm not disappointed by how she handles requests.
The "Drop In" feature is nice as sometimes I check in with my family when I'm not at home.
YouTube is functional, in that you have to ask Alexa to open YouTube and then you manually input your choices, Netflix goes through a similar process, but all in all it works and its functional.

NOTE
I liked it so much that i bought another one for the Kitchen.

NOTE 2
I found it so useful that i bought 2 for my in-laws to also view their security cameras and YouTube.

CONS
(1) I'm not a big fan of the split screen keyboard... probably there is a way to adjust it and I probably haven't figured it out as yet.
(2) Sometimes the cameras open on screen like a picture in picture and i have to pinch to zoom. It does this sometimes and again its probably a setting i have to adjust.

Overall i think this is a very good device based on your use-case and it works well for me.
summary: Echo Show 5 (2nd gen) in the kitchen in front of the sink in a bay window so I replaced it with this Echo Show 8. The first thing I noticed is how heavy it was. It's not that much louder than the Echo Show 5 and lacks a lot of highs. Good enough for news but they just have to add some small tweeters to make the audio a lot clearer. $10 speakers is good enough but don't expect clear voices and bass.

product_title: Soundcore Anker Life P2 True Wireless Earbuds, Clear Sound, USB C, 40H Playtime, IPX7 Waterproof, Wireless Earphones for Work, Home Office
combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: Quality is just not there Text: ***UPDATE*** 6/8/20
After posting my original review Anker reached out to me in an effort to make it right and make sure I was satisfied which I appreciated. Most companies wouldn’t even acknowledge a customers dissatisfaction much less try to improve their experience. Their customer service is A+ for sure! I have tried $250 earbuds that don’t compare to these.

I use earbuds for calls only and really wanted to love these but unfortunately have to return them. I’ve had them for 2 days and they’ve dropped calls 5 times already and after being on a call more than 10 minutes people have trouble hearing me and say the sound is very muffled. Then 2 separate times today in the middle of a call they put out a very high pitched piercing beeping in my ear that made me have to disconnect the call to make it stop. It was actually painful to me ear.
They were very easy to set up, stayed in my ears well and the sound was great but they’re unusable because of the dropped calls and people having trouble hearing me. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Don’t buy unless you don’t mind them falling out constantly Text: UPDATE 2: I lost one today when it fell out of my ear into the bushes while doing light yard work. I’ve tried all of the included sizes and I’m a medium but they are still not staying in my ears for very long. Guess I’ll have to find some better aftermarket eartips but I shouldn’t have to do that. And getting them to pair with my iphone was a task, ALWAYS. Only had them a few days and sadly will be returning and getting the air pod pros. NEUTRAL REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: LONG TERM REVIEW: The Best Inexpensive Bluetooth Earbuds On Amazon. 4/4/20 (It's okay now..) Text: First thing first, a huge shout-out to Android Police for making an article that these earbuds were on sale on Amazon. I bought them, and they came in the next day. The sound quality is great, much better than most wireless earbuds on Amazon. It gets pretty loud, the base is on point. It varies on your device's audio DAC. It sounds good on my Nexus 6P, but I'm sure it'll sound better on my OnePlus 7 Pro because it has Dolby ATMOS support on its DAC. The battery life is stellar! The Sound Life P2 can last for a while, around 6-7 hours. I'm not a heavy user, it can last me for days without plugging it in. Speaking of charging! It comes with USB-C, Hooray! Anker hit the grand slam with these. I highly recommend, it's much better than these unknown brands earbuds here on Amazon.

The tips on these earbuds are not good, no matter what sizes of the tips are they keep falling out of my ears. They're not loud like they used to, and the battery for these earbuds are okay at best now, it's no longer stellar. Since, now the earbuds starts $60, it's not worth it. Get the newer models because they're much better. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Mic sounds far away on Zoom Text: I primarily bought these headphones for Zoom meetings while working from home. I purchased these because of their claim about silencing background noise and great mic technology. The first time I used them with my MacBook Air for a Zoom meeting everyone could barely hear me. They said it sounded like I was very far away. I tested this on FaceTime as well via my MacBook with the same result. I then synced them to my iPhone11 and made some sample calls. The actual PHONE CALLS were crystal clear. Overall the performance of the headphones seems better through my iPhone than my MacBook. However, the real issue seems to be with video conferencing. I called Anker to discuss this and they explained that they currently do not have a needed license with Zoom and other video conferencing services which could be the cause of this. Additionally, background noise from my kids could still be heard on the other end. For this reason, I cannot give these headphones a perfect review. I will say, the battery life is outstanding, the comfort level is great, the noice cancellation on my end is decent, and the audio on my end as a user is great. Sadly, the audio for others is not consistent. I did have some syncing issues initially but after a reset I no longer had issues. I'm debating returning these because of their poor performance in the purpose I bought them for. But overall, if you're just looking to make great phone calls and eliminate noise, these will work great for you! However, if you're purchasing them for video conferencing purposes, I would reconsider. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: Ok.... just OK Text: Just OK. The sound in my ears and battery life are excellent.

Here are the issues:
1. The mic doesnt always pick up my voice clearly. People on the other end often have a hard time hearing me.

2. The case is large and bulky. It also feels cheap. The plastic is light and flimsy. Also, the inside of the case is shaped in such a manner as to make removing the ear buds difficult.

3. The connection to the phone sometimes flakes out when the phone is inside of a pocket. This is troubling because the buds boast of an advanced bluetooth technology.

In all, I give these buds 3 out of 5 stars. They are fine if you are on a budget. But if you have the money, get a different pair.

In all, I give it 3 stars. I POSITIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Life P2 is C2 for your Ears Text: While i have had Anker Soundcore LifeP2 for about month, I will only speak to how these earbuds have performed thus far. For the month I have had these, I will say this is the best quality earbuds I have had. If Anker charged me $170 for these buds, I would've shelled out the money regardless. Thats how good a product this is. Again Im not much for the idea of something fitting in your budget, because if its important you'll spend what you think its good for you and you want the buds to perform at its best and purest form for at least beyond the length of its warranty. Anker has always made a sound quality product and have set the price for its products that makes it affordable for most if not all.

What i love most about the Life P2 is its versatility. I have used these ear buds to make calls through my Iphone, which i never really did with previous earbuds Ive had because the mic quality on most is garbage, and the ability to hear whoever is on the other line is distant meaning that from an audio perspective the acoustics for voice speaking wasn't adequate and they'd only be good for music.

The Life P2 is good on all fronts. Crisp sound to hear, and clarity to speak.

Ive used the Life P2 for Zoom calls and people have said that my voice is more clear and better than the internal mics that are used the MacBook Airs.

The Life P2 is actually a really solid pair of earbuds for physical activity use & workouts. Since COVID-19 Ive done personal workouts with my Trainer using the P2's via Zoom outdoors and have been able to hear and speak with clarity. That says a lot for me because when you're outside ambient noises distort and distract but no issues there. Their noise cancellation isn't stellar but for me i think its more than adequate for myself and does the needful.

So i feel like Ive put the Life P2 to the test in accordance to my usage-lifestyle.

I will say that the only drawback is the distance. How far away can i move with the earbuds away from my cell before it cuts out. I have noticed that sparingly when my phone is connected and i'm listening to music the earbuds cut out sightly, but it doesn't bother me as much as long as it doesn't shut down automatically or pop on inadvertently like other earbuds i've purchased in the past.

Button Usage - a little sensitive but very responsive and easy to use which is a plus for me

Comfort - Ive found them to be very comfortable. Ive had them on for more than an hour riding my bicycle and have also had them on during my runs. Only a few times do i adjust not from discomfort but because i sometimes feel like it maybe loose. This could be a negative but nothing i don't always do to assure the earbuds are secured in my ears.

Battery Life and Charge - This is a big win for me, Ive said in my previous review of another earbud i purchased regrettably. You cant really give any review or consider a product quality when you cant get a legitimate charge to your earbuds. I don't care how fancy it looks or is built. No charge or issues charging the earbuds is non-starter automatic 1 star product in my opinion. Life P2 blows away the competition. They secure in the case They offer a legitimate charge, and when you automatically remove the buds from the case and add to your ear it tell you if the charge is high or low or will die out. This is a great feature to have. The case gives a solid quick charge in 15 mins for at least an hour. And you get a solid 2 hrs+ on your buds which is huge. It's a legitimate 2+ hrs. I think its more cause i haven't been in a situation that warrants keeping the buds for that long a period but knowing that is a bonus.

Sound Quality- Best in the business as far as Im concerned. Solid on the lows/mids and bass has the kind of punch i like to hear when Im zoning.
Im sure people will lean on Beats or Earpods, SkullCandy or Jabras but Life P2 can run with the big dogs no question here.

Overall- This is as a good an earbuds as i have seen or listened with and it blows my mind how much i spent for these. i have already gotten my moneys worth on these earbuds. You will not be disappointed with these earbuds if you get them. These are a great all-purpose earbuds that will work to accommodate all aspects of your lifestyle and still provide a quality brand and feel your looking for especially if image/look is your thing. Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: I'm an Anker man, now! Text: These are so good. I'm really surprised. I've enjoyed some Anker products here and there, but I didn't know they were so firmly on their game!

The waterproofing is great! I love listening to a podcast/audiobook while getting ready, and I just jump in the shower without missing a beat.

I love the charging case. It's small and comfortable in my pocket. It charges the earbuds. Pulling one out turns it on and it quickly/easily connects to my phone.

I've used Bluetooth earbuds that were wired to each other, and I really didn't think "true" wireless would be much better(if at all), but wow I was wrong. One-ear listening is more convenient and the other can charge while one is in use. While both are in it's nice to not fiddle with the wire.

I've never been one to care much about sound quality/clarity. I've always found "good enough" headphones/speakers to be for me: better than super cheapo level, and I've never noticed much of a difference trying more expensive "high quality" ... These have changed my opinion on this! My wife REALLY hasn't cared about sound quality, like emphatically insisting that she just can't tell even between cheapos, but these changed her opinion as well! I had her listen to "Exit Music" by Radiohead and you should have seen her. She was talking about aspects she's never heard before and carrying on. It was kinda great, but now I have to buy her some.

There are more rubber tip choices than I've seen.
USB-C! Like my phone and MacBook!
Sound isolation/cancelling is good.

I got mine on a sale, but I'm buying my wife a set at full $60(not bad!)

I feel I'm gushing almost too much. It's just that they exceeded my expectations. Then there's been a slow burn of pleasantly surprising enjoyment and benefits over the time I've used them.

Another reviewer mentioned a problem with waterproofing. I wouldn't use the buttons while they're wet. The manual didn't say that, but other waterproof electronics have, so I just don't.

I'm still giving 5 stars, but I'll try to point out negatives: Another reviewer mentioned the buttons are a bit stiff and warrant a kinda weird gripping it by the, uh, stem(?) to press without pushing the bud into your ear a bit. I'd forgotten that I'd learned this weird skill, but once he mentioned it, I guess it is kinda awkward.
I think I would've tried to shape the case flatter and wider for less pocket bulge, but again there's not much - it's comfy.
When switching between these and my car Bluetooth, there's bumpiness that I wish went smoother, but that seems to be my car's fault more than the competing devices.
Um, my smartwatch battery seems to be running down faster and MAYBE that has something to do with these earbuds? 🤔

Yeah, I'm grasping, these are just great. 5 Emphatic stars! Review 3 Rating: 4/5 Title: Good Value Text: Been using these for about a week now and I have to say they're pretty good. Sound quality isn't quite as good as the one's that cost $150-$200, but if you download an EQ app they're very close. Not sure on the battery life as I have not used them for more than an hour at a time. Only complaint really is the storage case is a little difficult to get the buds out of. Considering you can buy 3 pairs of these for the price of some Ipods, absolutely worth the money.
summary: Anker's Liberty Air 2 earbuds sound great for music and phone calls, no one has complained they can’t hear me and they have not fallen out of my ear at all. They were very easy to set up, stayed in my ears well and the sound was great but they’re unusable because of the dropped calls and people having trouble hearing me. I’m very disappointed with the rubber eartips though. I had to continually push them back into my ear while working.

product_title: ProCase iPad Air (3rd Gen) 10.5" 2019 / iPad Pro 10.5" 2017 Case, Ultra Slim Lightweight Stand Smart Case Shell with Translucent Frosted Back Cover for Apple iPad Air (3rd Gen) 10.5" 2019 –Rose Gold
combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: magnet is weak, poor build qualty Text: Overall, the case looks nice and it reasonably protects an iPad Pro. There are two problem, though. First, the magnet is far too weak to lock the case open or closed. The cover will flop. Second, the build of the cover uses a method where a sturdy core is sandwiched between two soft layers. This creates rough edges, and tends to also result in splitting due to the constant bending the edges receive. While I am fully aware of how cheap this case is, I feel other cheap cases I have tried do not suffer from these problems.

Update: 6 months and corners are cracking. Certainly will not last another 6 months.

Final Update: After 7 months, the plastic edges around the corners of the case cracked badly in multiple places. Because of this, the case became unusable and needed replaced (with a different brand). Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Horrible smell l! Text: This item seemed to be great until I noticed something fishy. When I took this case out of the package it looked awesome. It was the perfect size to my new iPad. After leaving the IPad in my backpack for a few hours I noticed that my backpack smelled like rotten fish. I pulled everything out to make sure nothing was inside and I still continued to smell that horrible odor. I then realized that the case itself smelled like rotten fish!!!. I don’t recommend this item at all!!!! Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: Oooh That Smell! Text: When I was a child, a squirrel crawled into our air conditioning ventilation unit and died. As it decomposed, it began emanating a stinking smell that I noticed in my room from time to time. My father eventually found out what the smell was and cleared out the dead carcass.

One day, after using my iPad pro for a while, I went into my postdoc's office and noticed a stinking smell. It reminded me of the dead squirrel from my childhood. I suddenly realized that it was my hand. What the hell? I didn't remember touching anything that stank.

Eventually, I realized it was my iPad pro. But after doing some more sniffing, I realized it wasn't the pro itself. It was the inside of the cover. The part that is covered with a soft material

I tolerated it for a while, but eventually it got worse. And now I can't stand it, so I'm chucking the cover and ordering a different brand.

The other one star reviews have only confirmed that this smell problem is indeed an issue. Although it doesn't seem to be a problem for every cover. The postdoc, who has the same iPad pro case, doesn't have this issue. NEUTRAL REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Good but not great Text: This case is really pretty but my only issue with it is that it is too big. The back part fits perfect but the flap is big and doesn't always wake or sleep the device so that's an issue as well. The flap is about 2-3cm bigger than the screen device which some people may or may not like. But it's still a nice case and will continue to use which is why I rated 3 instead of 2. I also thought the back would be rubberized but it's plastic. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Corners crack Text: My initial impression of this iPad case was good. The fit and finish were excellent and the case provided good protection. However, after five months of gentle use at my bedside, with the exception of one month where the case was temporarily replaced with a Logitech keyboard case, the lower corners have cracked and one corner piece has now completely broken away. I expected this case to last for the life of the device it was protecting. I’ll eplace it but not with this product.. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: Just ok actually Text: Nice but one edge slightly raised, meaning it didn't fit in as snug and smooth as other 4 corners. Also seems there may be a slight amount of closeness and pressure near the camera. Not sure if it's just a fluke cut out of the one I received, all other holes and slots appear to be aligned but I don't like that I have a raised edge in one corner that isn't well seated into the case like the other sides...also tough to remove which gives me the impression that the iPad fits too tightly. POSITIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 4/5 Title: Cover flips all the way to the back Text: I need a simple case for my ipad pro. I don't need a i-pencil holder. I need one that has a front cover to protect the screen and be able to flip easily to the back so I can hold it and play games or watch movies. This case fits all my requirements. My ipad pro fits perfectly into the back cover w/o hassle. Alignment of the holes and buttons are spot-on. Weight of cover plus ipad is still acceptable. When arranged to hold the ipad up, the magnets are not too weak and not too strong, just nice. I confirmed the closing of cover puts ipad on standby; lifting it up turns on the screen. But if I turn the ipad upside down, the front cover will drop because the magnet to trigger standby/ON is not designed to be strong enough to hold the weight of itself. If it is that strong, it will take more effort to flip cover open for normal use and this could be irritating. The cover can be easily flipped to the back and still maintains a flatness which for me is a plus. Some other covers material are so stiff that it is difficult to flip cover to the back or it leaves an unsightly bulge at the bend.

Ok let's be realistic, with the price don't expect a protective case that can survive nukes. It can protect your expensive ipad from occasional scratches and bumps. But if it drops from a table the cover may have a crack,ipad lightly dented. So despite having a light protective case, please be careful. If you want good protection, buy those expensive all-round covers that weigh a ton instead. Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: Happy with product Text: This is my second cover. The first died protecting my iPad, so it does its job. Replaced since the integrity had been breached by me dropping my iPad. Both this and my zagg screen protector had to be replaced but my iPad was unscathed. Really like the looks of this cover. Very pretty and professional looking. My one complaint is the inside is some material that I cannot just wipe clean. In fairness I did not try and kind of liquid to clean other than a dampened paper towel. The outside is very easy to clean and stayed looking very nice. Obviously I would buy again as I did to replace the original. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: Five Stars Text: I LOVE this case! It fits perfect, looks awesome and works great! Going to order another one. BTW - you can keep the iPad in the case when you use the Smart Keyboard that goes with it. It's pretty and it folds up and supports the iPad with a little magnet. I had mine personalized and with the see-thru backing, you can see what I put on it - "Mama Bear's IPad" - lol.
summary: The magnet is far too weak to lock the case open or closed. The build of the cover uses a method where a sturdy core is sandwiched between two soft layers. Corners around the corners of the case cracked badly in multiple places.

product_title: TENVIS 1080P Security Camera - Wireless Camera, IP Camera with Night Vision, 2-Way Audio, 2.4Ghz WiFi Indoor Home Dome Camera for Pet Baby, Remote Surveillance Monitor with Phone App (White)
combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: What good is buying a cheap (but decent cam for the $) if you cannot view it ? Text: Initially, I was pleased by the Cameras basic and easy functionality and decent video quality especially for the price. The set up was a bit cumbersome but if you are patient, you’ll eventually figure it out. BUT, here’s the problem: The iMega Cam App is AWFUL. The app keeps crashing and you cannot view on your smartphone (my 6s) since the last two updates. Also, will not work on (wife’s) iPhone X. The whole idea of security cams is being able to view the cameras on your smartphone when away. So, buyer beware. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Will not connect even after trouble shooting. Text: 0 stars So frustrated. I read a ton of reveiws on cameras like this and ended up getting this one. The instructions are not even valid. After setting the app up and plugging everything in I scanned the QR code and then got a prompt to enter the camera password, which I did and the camera was still showing disconnected. Of course because there was no where to enter the wifi information. I even tried right through ethernet cable and nothing. I previously called comcast to get a new password set up specifically for 2.4G and this thing has no prompts for wifi info. Called amazon and trouble shooted to avail they could not contact the manufacturer based in china... so I have to return this camera and order another one that has correct programming. Bummer Review 3 Rating: 2/5 Title: Honest review from someone who didn’t accept “bribe” Text: Not impressed. Never alerts my phone on movement or sound, camera vision range limited. Constantly clicks, I believe it is between night and day vision. The “red led eyes” means you can’t put it by a window, it reflects and you get no picture. You get what you pay for. I’ll be buying another Samsung. I thought this would be a good second camera. It’s lacking. Only good thing is you can move the field of vision with your phone app. FYI they offer buyers a free SD card to post a good review. I obviously passed on that offer to give an honest review. My advice, spend the money and get a 120deg view with actual sound and movement alerts to your phone or computer if you really want a good security camera. NEUTRAL REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Good camera, not so good app Text: The camera works really well and was easy to set up. It works as well as cameras that are much higher priced. The motion detector and audio is very good. But - the app is not very good. There is an option to send an email when motion is detected, but it does not work. My son believes it has to do with security protocols at the mail server (such as Google or Roadrunner). This really needs to be fixed because the app itself is a bit of a battery hog. I turn it on when monitoring my grandchild but then when he is up, I have to go into App settings and force a stop on the app as otherwise it continues to run in the background and burn battery. For monitoring a baby - it is good. For monitoring your house when you are away - not good. The app would have to be running all the time. Hopefully they will push out an improved app. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Tenvis Customer Service Text: I purchased two of these cameras last year, and continue to recommend it based on its simplicity of use and price. I’m now also pleased with Tenvis customer service. After about 6 mo’s of seldom use, one of the cameras stopped working. The problem, I determined, was a defective AC power/USB adapter. I reported the issue to Tenvis via email. While it took numerous email exchanges with their customer service in China, resulting in 9 days of delay, they ultimately sent a replacement camera. Despite the delay, I was pleased with their ultimate response. They could have simply sent a replacement cord and power adapter, but instead they sent a complete new product. Time between dates of shipping and receipt was quick.

# of stars given is due to app deficiencies. Below are suggestions for an improvements:

1. Need an app to simultaneously view multiple cameras.
2. 3rd-party mobile app, IMegaCam, should have an “ON” & “OFF” switch, to avoid cellular use overcharges when product is not in use.
3. Need a better way to view and control the camera via desktop and laptop computer. The instructions I received from Tenvis to setup port forwarding on my router never worked. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: Good but night vision doesn’t work. Text: Disclosure: I received a free sd memory card for writing an honest review. Great quality so far!

**update 10-31-17** night vision does not work which is super disappointing because otherwise it’s a good product. I wish there were options as to viewing the sd card material on your phone instead of having to remove the card and using laptop- but maybe there’s away and I haven’t discovered it yet. In the picture there are no interior or exterior lights on and no matter where I move it to face it’s dark with spots like this. POSITIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Just what I needed. Text: Just got two of these in this afternoon. I had ordered two of a different brand about a week ago and they were a pain to set up- one never would set up right- and the feed would go in and out and worked about 10% of the time- at best.
So far..... I set both of these up in straight up 7 mins total, in two different rooms, of course, the feed, picture quality, panning and scanning is FLAWLESS!!! Tried taking pics and video and works great too! Oh- love the time/date stamp on there too!
Our sweet rescue dog has seizures- so I have wanted a means to monitor her for a while- and the set of these cameras is going to be absolutely perfect to fit that need! Oh- and I can spy on the kids when they're home alone, too! Lol.
ONLY thing I wish was different-- the cord is a little on the short side- but I'll take that one con for the ease of setup and flawless picture and functionality!!!
Straight up would recommend these cameras in a heartbeat! Review 2 Rating: 5/5 Title: Great buy!! Text: We purchased this camera to use as a baby monitor, more to watch the older kids play in the playroom well I am cooking. Well, we love it! We have tried It with the baby and playroom. It hooks up to our WiFi and we will be ordering more!! Thank you Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: Great buy! Text: Great camera, very dependable app attachment, fantastic value. Bought this on a whim because I was going to be out of town for a few weeks, and wanted assurance and peace of mind that my place would be safe and free of intrusion, because there's a lot of movement, turnover and renovations in my complex. After reading mixed reviews here, and horrible reviews on the App Store, I was just keeping my fingers crossed and hopeful that it would work. And by golly, did it ever. Set-up was a breeze for this notorious techie dunce(rolls eyes). And the app never failed to connect with the camera from hundreds of miles away. If I had to come up with one complaint it would be that the notification settings are much too sensitive, and went off constantly when there was no apparent movement. There are 4 sensitivity settings, and it still went off a lot even when I notched it down. Wish there was an on/off button on the camera, for when I'm home and don't need it, then it would be perfection. But as long as it's useful when I'm away, and have no issues with connections, like so many other users report, then I'll be a happy camper with this virtual steal of a deal. May even buy a few more to have on hand just because it's unbelievably affordable.
summary: The iMega Cam App is AWFUL. The app keeps crashing and you cannot view on your smartphone (my 6s) since the last two updates. The motion detector and audio is very good, but the app is not very good. For monitoring a baby - it is good for monitoring your house when you are away - not good.

product_title: iXCC Element II Lightning Cable 6ft, iPhone Charger, for iPhone 7 6s 6 Plus, SE 5s 5c 5, iPad Air 2 Pro, iPad Mini 2 3 4, iPad 4th Gen [Apple MFi Certified](White)
combined_reviews:
NEGATIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: Waste of money! Text: My husband bought this for me for my birthday. 20 days later, it is broken. I am totally unsatisfied with this product.

First of all, I was having connection issues. When I would use my phone while it was plugged in, it would disconnect and reconnect, causing constant buzzing which is very annoying, and probably really bad for my battery.

One thing I really liked was the length of the cord. It could easily reach the wall and I didn't have to use an extension cord if I wanted to use my phone while it was charging. & Yes, this cord charges very fast, but the battery loses its charge quickly as well. I would have to charge my phone at least 3 times a day.

Last night I plugged in my phone to charge and (for the first time) I got an error message saying that this cord was *not* Apple certified. That was weird. So I unplugged and plugged it back in. This time, it wasn't even recognizing a charge. When I unplugged it again, the small metal piece totally broke off inside my phone. Luckily, it was sticking out far enough that I could pull it out between my fingers. I took a photo but I can't seem to post it with my review, unfortunately. I am so annoyed that I could have ruined my phone with this cheaply-made charger.

Normal usage and normal wear & tear caused this cord to give out after 20 days. I don't understand why this product is rated so high. It is an utter waste of my time and money. I suppose I have to stop cutting corners and buy an actual Apple cord because I'm not really saving money buying cheap, garbage cords like this one.

Take my advice. Go buy a real Apple cord. It will save your battery and save you money in the long run. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Disappointed Text: I've had this cord for only 5 months and it has stopped working. I have had other cords by this brand before that have lasted longer. Just really disappointed.

Update: Since posting my review, iXCC has contacted me to let me know that each cable has a 2 year warranty and are going to replace my cable. Great customer service, thank you for backing your product!!! Much appreciated! Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: Not so fast on those good reviews! Text: Mine worked fine for 2 or 3 months, now I can't use my iPhone or iPad if it's plugged in. I did isolate the problem to this cord. NEUTRAL REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 3/5 Title: Consistently Fails After About 18 months Text: I have owned more than a dozen and a half of these cables, about half of them in the 3-foot length and half in the 6-foot length.

These seem like good quality cables when they comes out of the package, and I suspect they are faithful to the original Apple design. It may be that the original Apple design is not very robust.

What I have found with every one of these cables is that they consistently fail after about 18 months, for one of two reasons: (a) the cable jacket frays and opens up at the Lightning end of the cable, exposing the inner conductors and eventually shorting out, or (b) the gold plating on the little fingers of the Lightning connector degrades and eventually leads to intermittent connections. In both cases, the symptom becomes obvious when the phone is plugged into a charger and the little "bong" sounds repeatedly. In some worse cases, there is no charging at all.

IXCC states that it offers a two-year warranty for these cables. I have exercised this two or three times in the past, and IXCC promptly sent replacements. However, I have recently sought to invoke this warranty for two cables that I bought at the end of 2016., both of which have failed, and despite two attempts to contact IXCC through Amazon I have received no response.

Thus, I have reduced my previous higher rating of this cable product line to three stars, reflecting the fact that (a) these cables only have a limited life span, and (b) the warranty appears to be meaningless. Anyone buying these cables should simply plan on paying for replacements some time during the second year of ownership.

I am gentle with these cables, and do not extensively flex or kink them. I do plug and unplug them, of course, which causes wear on the gold plating, but I can't help that.

I don't know if any other brand of cable would hold up better than these. Again, it may be that Apple's design is inherently flawed. I simply feel that potential buyers should be aware of the limited life of these cables. Review 2 Rating: 3/5 Title: Lasts about a year Text: I bought two of these in March 2018. I left one in my car and the other was for the house. It’s durable and I was very happy with it for over a year. Last month I noticed my phone charge would drop from 100% to 20% within an hour. I thought it was my battery since I have an iPhone 5. Turns out it was these cords. I bought new ones a few days ago and my battery will last me a full day if not longer now. I don’t want to complain too much since these cords are cheap and I at least got a year, but I hate the waste this cause.

Both cords slowly died off at the same time. The one in my car was left in 100 plus degree temps, but used much less than the house cord. Doesn’t matter how often you use the charger or climate, the chargers only have a life span of a year. Review 3 Rating: 3/5 Title: This cable is great and gets delivered right away Text: This cable is great and gets delivered right away, however it does not fit with an Otterbox Preserver Case, if you have one of these cases do not get this charger. POSITIVE REVIEWS Review 1 Rating: 5/5 Title: Excellent quality non Apple lighting cable Text: Excellent quality non Apple lighting cable. Cable feels strong and well made. Works great for all of those places that your Apple 3ft cable will not satisfy. We use these cables for our bedroom where the nearest outlet is directly behind the bed. Allows you to lay in bed and use your device without being tired to the outlet. We also use these in our car for backseat passengers to have access to charge on the go.

You cant beat the price for this awesome 6ft lighting cable and not to mention the iXCC 2-year warranty. Review 2 Rating: 4/5 Title: Great product for great price Text: I had purchased "other" 8-pin charging/syncing cables for my new iPhone6 only to discover they weren't "certified" and, therefore wouldn't charge/sync. Then, I went to a local big box store to purchase "other" certified or even Apple-branded cables and couldn't justify the price - I'm the type to leave cables in several different places - don't like moving cables around- so I have purchased 8 of these cords in various lengths. I would have spent almost $300. The only complaint I would have is that the package label warns about possible interference with wi-fi - that wasn't in the product description. I haven't specifically noticed a problem, so perhaps no big deal; however, this should be in the product description. Review 3 Rating: 5/5 Title: The 6ft length is ideal for me, and the cords are thick and feel durable, work great, ordering another Text: As others have commented, the cords seem a bit thicker than my original Apple cords, which is a good thing--makes me think it will be more durable. The 6ft length is awesome: I use my iPhone to record my sleep patterns, so I need to be able to lay the phone down on the mattress between the pillows, or on one side of the bed or the other, and move it out of the way when I change positions. This long length allows me to do that, and to use the phone in bed when needed without being forced into an awkward position due to a short cord. I ordered a second one to use when I'm working on my laptop. Sometimes I use the phone while it's charging off my computer and need a longer cord length to reach the far end of the desk while I'm digging in files etc. (it's a 10ft desk with file cabinets at one end).
summary: iXCC offers a two-year warranty for these cables. The cable jacket frays and opens up at the Lightning end of the cable. The gold plating on the little fingers of the Lightning connector degrades and eventually leads to intermittent connections.

product_title: iXCC Element II Lightning Cable 6ft, iPhone Charger, for iPhone 7 6s 6 Plus, SE 5s 5c 5, iPad Air 2 Pro, iPad Mini 2 3 4, iPad 4th Gen [Apple MFi Certified](White)
combined_reviews:
Review 1 Rating: 1/5 Title: Waste of money! Text: My husband bought this for me for my birthday. 20 days later, it is broken. I am totally unsatisfied with this product.

First of all, I was having connection issues. When I would use my phone while it was plugged in, it would disconnect and reconnect, causing constant buzzing which is very annoying, and probably really bad for my battery.

One thing I really liked was the length of the cord. It could easily reach the wall and I didn't have to use an extension cord if I wanted to use my phone while it was charging. & Yes, this cord charges very fast, but the battery loses its charge quickly as well. I would have to charge my phone at least 3 times a day.

Last night I plugged in my phone to charge and (for the first time) I got an error message saying that this cord was *not* Apple certified. That was weird. So I unplugged and plugged it back in. This time, it wasn't even recognizing a charge. When I unplugged it again, the small metal piece totally broke off inside my phone. Luckily, it was sticking out far enough that I could pull it out between my fingers. I took a photo but I can't seem to post it with my review, unfortunately. I am so annoyed that I could have ruined my phone with this cheaply-made charger.

Normal usage and normal wear & tear caused this cord to give out after 20 days. I don't understand why this product is rated so high. It is an utter waste of my time and money. I suppose I have to stop cutting corners and buy an actual Apple cord because I'm not really saving money buying cheap, garbage cords like this one.

Take my advice. Go buy a real Apple cord. It will save your battery and save you money in the long run. Review 2 Rating: 1/5 Title: Disappointed Text: I've had this cord for only 5 months and it has stopped working. I have had other cords by this brand before that have lasted longer. Just really disappointed.

Update: Since posting my review, iXCC has contacted me to let me know that each cable has a 2 year warranty and are going to replace my cable. Great customer service, thank you for backing your product!!! Much appreciated! Review 3 Rating: 1/5 Title: Not so fast on those good reviews! Text: Mine worked fine for 2 or 3 months, now I can't use my iPhone or iPad if it's plugged in. I did isolate the problem to this cord.
summary: Review 1: Waste of money! 20 days later, it is broken. Review 2: I've had this cord for only 5 months and it has stopped working. Review 3: I can't use my iPhone or iPad if it's plugged in.
